bullets.text: An Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo is spreading faster than initially thought, with the World Health Organization (WHO) acknowledging they are playing catch-up and considering experimental vaccines as cases rise.
Estonia claims a NATO jet shot down a drone over its territory, suspected to be a Ukrainian projectile diverted by Russian electronic jamming.

The US government has announced it will admit 10,000 more white South Africans, claiming an "emergency refugee situation," amidst repeated unsubstantiated claims by Trump about a genocide against white Afrikaners.
The federal government plans to spend $11 billion on modernising Australia's Collins-class submarine fleet.

CSIRO is reportedly planning to dismiss a third of its team working on the national climate model, which scientists warn could hinder Australia's ability to provide climate projections for global reports.
Former NT child protection staff have spoken out about the system's scrutiny following the alleged murder of a five-year-old in Alice Springs.

Investors are concerned about a potential resurgence in inflation, leading to a significant selloff in the $31 trillion US government debt market and pushing bond yields to multi-year highs, with the 30-year yield reaching its highest since 2007.
US stocks have fallen for three consecutive days due to rising bond yields and inflation concerns, with semiconductor shares notably declining.
Google plans to release smart glasses and integrate AI 'agents' into its search engine, powered by its new Gemini model, aiming to compete with companies like Anthropic and OpenAI.
